rulings.Case Title: PACIFIC POLK PROPERTIES, LLC, et al VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A CHARTERED et al (CEQA Case)
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Or Tax Costs
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, June 27, 2014, Line 1, PLAINTIFF PACIFIC POLK PROPERTIES, LLC'S Motion To Strike Or Tax Costs. Plaintiffs' motion to strike Defendants' memorandum of costs in its entirety is denied. Plaintiffs have not set forth sufficient evidence establishing that Defendants waived their statutory right to recover costs. Plaintiffs' motion to tax costs is granted in part and denied in part. Defendants may recover the following costs: (1) Filing and Motion fees in the amount of $280.00; (2) Service of Process Costs in the amount of $388.50; (3) Court Reporter Costs in the amount of $565.00; and (4) Administrative Record Costs in the amount of $5,215.36. All other costs are stricken. Plaintiffs shall pay Defendants costs in the amount of $6,448.86 within 30 days of entry of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: JOANNA FULL VS. BAY AREA RAPID TRANSIT ("BART") et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Compliance With Deposition Subpoena; Request For Order Awarding Monetary Sanctions Against California Pacific Medical Center In The Amount Of $8,330.50
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, June 27, 2014, Line 1, DEFENDANT BAY AREA RAPID TRANSIT's ("BART") Motion To Compel Compliance With Deposition Subpoena; Request For Order Awarding Monetary Sanctions Against California Pacific Medical Center In The Amount Of $8,330.50 Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Motion is granted. Howell v. Hamilton Meats (2011) 52 Cal. 4th 541 does not directly govern this discovery issue, and it does not appear that any California case has determined whether a party may take discovery to attempt to elicit evidence on the reasonable value of medical services where such services have been paid for at a negotiated rate by an insurer. Howell does state: "Thus the general rule under the Restatement, as well as California law, is that a personal injury plaintiff may recover the lesser of (a) the amount paid or incurred for medical services, and (b) the reasonable value of the services." This court takes that to mean that a party may take discovery on the reasonable value of services. Subject to a stipulated protective order to be negotiated, CPMC shall, no later than July 25, 2014 appear at its deposition and produce the documents requested in the deposition subpoena. Whether or not the adduced discovery is admissible at trial is left for the trial judge. No sanctions. All parties acted with substantially justification. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JOSE MARTINEZ VS. JOHN C. JEW et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Order Imposing Terminating Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Friday, June 27, 2014, Line 3, DEFENDANT JOHN JEW's Motion For Order Imposing Terminating Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion is denied. The pro per Plaintiff's failure to provide responses to supplemental discovery and his failure to pay $360 in sanctions does not warrant terminating sanctions at this time. However, if Plaintiff does not respond to the supplemental discovery within 20 days of this Order, he risks terminating sanctions. Defendant shall serve this Order to Plaintiff along with a cover letter advising him in simple understandable language that Plaintiff risks losing the case altogether if he does not timely respond to the supplemental discovery. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: ROCKY POINT PARTNERS, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED VS. YAT-PANG AU et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, June 27, 2014, Line 6, DEFENDANTS YAT-PANG AU, RUSSIAN HILL GROUP, LLC, NOB HILL VISTAS, LLC, EUREKA PARK, LLC, YAT-HON AU, YAT-GAI AU, PARNASSUS PLACE, LLC, RUSSIAN HILL ASSOCIATES, LLC'S DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained in part and overruled in part. As to the causes of action for breach of contract and common counts, the demurrer is overruled. "A licensed broker may be able under appropriate circumstances to recover under an oral agreement or in quantum meruit for certain services other than the purchase, sale, or leasing of real property." (Phillippe v. Shapell Industries (1987) 43 Cal.3d 1247, 1255.) "A valid oral argument could be made for the special services alleged, consisting of the surveys made, the furnishing of plans, specifications and estimates¿" (Owen v. Nat'l Container Corp. of Cal. (1952) 115 Cal.App.2d 21, 26.) The Second Amended Complaint ("SAC") adequately alleges that Telegraph Hill Properties performed services beyond negotiating and effecting a purchase of real property. The SAC alleges various building and approval services performed by Plaintiff, including developing plans for improvement, obtaining bids for the improvement work, assisting in the selection of architects and engineers, obtaining permits, hiring and consulting with land surveyors, and preparing budgets. (SAC, pars. 20-21.) As to the cause of action for fraud, the demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as unopposed. Additionally, the Order sustaining the demurrer to the First Amended Complaint gave Plaintiff leave to amend to plead facts with the requisite specificity for a fraud claim. Plaintiff added no additional facts to support this cause of action in the SAC. As to Defendants' request for judicial notice, the Court takes judicial notice of the existence of the documents pursuant to Evid. Code sec. 452(d), but does not judicially notice the content of the documents. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: FLOR KHAN VS. URBAN OUTFITTERS WEST, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, June 27, 2014, Line 11, DEFENDANT URBAN OUTFITTERS WEST, LLC'S Motion To Stay Proceedings. Granted. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: ZHOU HUA RONG et al VS. LARRY WEI et al

rulings.Case Title: CONSUMER ADVOCACY GROUP INC VS. NATIONAL STORES INC, DBA FALLAS DISCOUNT STORES, et al***TRANSFERRED TO LA COUNTY***

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Friday, June 27, 2014, Line 13, DEFENDANT NATIONAL STORES INC.'S Motion For An Order Transferring Venue Of Action To The Central District For The County Of L.A. And Request For Award Of Attorneys Fees Of $3437.50 Against Yeroushalmi & Assoc. Granted. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
